title: DiffusionGemma: Faster Text Generation
note: Primary source — announcement with speed benchmarks and architecture overview. Section 1–3 전반.

title: diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it: Model Card
note: Primary source — official benchmark table (MMLU Pro 77.6%, AIME 69.1%, GPQA 73.2% etc.). Section 3 품질 격차.

title: DiffusionGemma Documentation
note: Official technical documentation — architecture, inference settings, denoising parameters.

title: DiffusionGemma — The Developer Guide
note: Developer guide — Sudoku fine-tuning demo, adaptive denoising steps (12–16), temperature schedule.

title: Run DiffusionGemma Locally on RTX
note: Section 3 inference economics; DGX Station (2,000 tok/s) and DGX Spark (150 tok/s) figures.

title: DiffusionGemma Throughput Benchmarks
note: Day-zero native support; H100 FP8 1,008 tok/s, H200 1,288 tok/s; 5–6× vs AR Gemma 4.

title: Large Language Diffusion Models
container-title: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2025)
note: NeurIPS 2025 Oral. Section 4–5: data efficiency evidence — LLaDA 2.3T tokens ≈ LLaMA3 15T on MMLU (65.9 vs 65.4).

title: Mercury: A Commercial-Scale Diffusion Language Model
note: arXiv:2506.17298. Mercury 2: 1,196 tok/s ($0.25/M input) — commercial diffusion LLM benchmark.

title: Performance Characterization of Diffusion Language Models
note: arXiv:2510.04146. Roofline analysis — diffusion is compute-bound vs AR memory-bound at batch=1.

title: Fast-dLLM: Efficient Decoding for Diffusion Language Models
note: arXiv:2509.26328. Fast-dLLM v2 — 1.5–1.8× at batch=64 (high-batch advantage retained with acceleration stack).

title: Diffusion Language Models are Super Data Learners
note: arXiv:2511.03276. Data-efficiency advantage of diffusion LMs in data-constrained settings.

title: Mind the Memory Gap: GPU FLOPS vs. Bandwidth Growth
note: arXiv:2503.08311. Hardware trends underlying compute-bound vs bandwidth-bound shift.

title: LLaDA 2.0: Scaling Diffusion Language Models
note: arXiv:2512.15745. LLaDA 2.0-flash: first 100B MoE diffusion LLM, 535 tok/s (Ant Group self-reported; not independently verified).

title: Simple and Effective Masked Diffusion Language Models
note: arXiv:2406.07524. MDLM — masked diffusion, direct genealogical predecessor.

title: Discrete Diffusion Language Modeling by Estimating the Ratios of the Data Distribution
container-title: Proceedings of the 41st International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2024)
note: arXiv:2310.16834. SEDD — score entropy discrete diffusion, genealogy.

title: Block Diffusion: Interpolating Between Autoregressive and Diffusion Language Models
note: Block Diffusion sampling — batch crossover analysis (AR wins after batch=2–4). Genealogy section.

title: Diffusion Beats Autoregressive in Data-Constrained Settings
note: Data-constrained efficiency argument for diffusion LMs. Section 4 data quality basis.
